jokes.text: You want to go to the Penn Station Kmart with me and then watch Tootsie?
jokes.setup: Jack asking about Liz's Christmas Eve plans
jokes.punchline: The combination of Penn Station Kmart (depressing location) with Tootsie (random movie choice) as holiday plans
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon character moment - the specificity of Penn Station Kmart elevates it beyond generic 'sad Christmas' setup

jokes.text: My aunt Linda is bringing her new boyfriend, who is neither her age nor her race. And her ex-husband will also be there with his date, alcoholism.
jokes.setup: Describing family dysfunction with boyfriend issues
jokes.punchline: Personifying alcoholism as the ex-husband's date
jokes.explanation: Brilliant structure - sets up expectation of person, delivers addiction. Perfect economy and highly quotable.

jokes.text: I swoop in the next day for presents and pie.
jokes.setup: Avoiding family drama on Christmas
jokes.punchline: The calculated, vulture-like 'swoop' for material benefits only
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's practical selfishness, but not as sharp as surrounding jokes

jokes.text: Because my youthful energy makes her feel young? No, because she views you as a peer she can complain with about how no one wears pantyhose any longer.
jokes.setup: Liz assuming positive reason for Colleen wanting to see her
jokes.punchline: Jack revealing it's because Colleen sees Liz as equally old and crotchety
jokes.explanation: Great character dynamic - Jack's brutal honesty about both women's antiquated sensibilities

jokes.text: Ah, how are we supposed to conceal our spider veins? Bare-legged at Christmas.
jokes.setup: Pantyhose conversation
jokes.punchline: Liz immediately proving Jack's point by complaining about spider veins
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment where Liz immediately validates Jack's insult by acting exactly as described

jokes.text: As an actor, it is my job to tell the truth, hold the mirror to humanity, and sell proactiv.
jokes.setup: Tracy giving high-minded speech about acting responsibilities
jokes.punchline: Ending with crass commercial endorsement
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy Jordan - mixing pretentious artistic integrity with shameless commercialism in perfect contradiction

jokes.text: Ever since Tracy got nominated for a Golden Globe, he thinks he's Sean Penn. Well, they have both had screaming fights with Wyclef Jean.
jokes.setup: Tracy acting pretentious like Sean Penn
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific shared experience with Wyclef Jean
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist specificity - the Wyclef Jean detail is so random and specific it becomes hilarious

jokes.text: Also replicas of the David urinate vodka.
jokes.setup: Description of fancy New Year's party elements
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'David' (likely referring to Michelangelo's David statue) as 'urinate'
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna malapropism but not as clever as her best wordplay moments

jokes.text: The party or Paul? The party, Liz.
jokes.setup: Jenna seeming upset about missing party without Paul
jokes.punchline: Clarifying she only cares about the party, not the relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna selfishness - prioritizing party over relationship with brutal honesty

jokes.text: Happy holidays is what terrorists say.
jokes.setup: Reading Christmas card message
jokes.punchline: Jack's extreme conservative take on holiday greetings
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack Donaghy conservative extreme - fits character perfectly but joke is fairly predictable

jokes.text: Oh, it's just Liz. Why do people always say that?
jokes.setup: Someone saying 'it's just Liz' dismissively
jokes.punchline: Liz's sad recognition that this happens frequently
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's low status but not particularly clever structure

jokes.text: Avery is keeping her pregnancy a secret at work, so she's been carrying around large objects whenever she's in the building.
jokes.setup: Hiding pregnancy at work
jokes.punchline: Absurd solution of constantly carrying large objects
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy concept - absurd but somehow logical solution to pregnancy concealment

jokes.text: That's why I like dating older men. Their parents are usually dead or senile, so there's never an argument about the holidays.
jokes.setup: Discussion about family holiday obligations
jokes.punchline: Coldly pragmatic view of dating older men for convenience
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Avery logic - brutally practical and character-specific reasoning that's both shocking and logical

jokes.text: I have been watching The Mentalist a lot lately, because my tv's on CBS, and I lost my remote. I think I've become a body-language expert.
jokes.setup: Liz claiming expertise in body language
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous origin story of expertise from being stuck on CBS
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Liz logic - the escalation from 'lost remote' to 'expert' via CBS shows is perfectly absurd and character-specific

jokes.text: For instance, I can now tell that Jack wants to kill the person to his right.
jokes.setup: Liz demonstrating her new body language expertise
jokes.punchline: Obvious observation about Jack's murderous intent (likely toward his mother)
jokes.explanation: Solid followup but less funny than the setup - the punchline is too obvious given Jack-Colleen dynamics

jokes.text: We Donaghys believe that, when there's something at all delicate to talk about, it is best to suppress it until it erupts into a fistfight at a church barbecue.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining family communication philosophy
jokes.punchline: The specific, absurd outcome of 'fistfight at church barbecue'
jokes.explanation: Exceptional character writing - the specificity of 'church barbecue' makes it both classy and violent, perfect for Donaghys

jokes.text: The symbol on the Jessup family crest is a knight refusing to talk about his feelings
jokes.setup: Liz joining conversation about family emotional dysfunction
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific family crest depicting emotional repression
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual concept - family crest heraldry combined with modern psychological dysfunction is inspired

jokes.text: I am 'The Mentaliz.'
jokes.setup: Liz showing off her deductive abilities
jokes.punchline: Mangling 'The Mentalist' into street slang version
jokes.explanation: Decent callback but the wordplay isn't clever enough to elevate it significantly

jokes.text: Thanks, K-L-M-N-O-P.
jokes.setup: Kenneth delivering something to Jack
jokes.punchline: Jack calling Kenneth by alphabet letters instead of his name
jokes.explanation: Good running gag showing Jack's dismissive attitude toward Kenneth, economical and character-appropriate

jokes.text: Dearly beloved, we are gathered here to celebrate the life of Althea Chunk. Damn, that's funky. Obesity is killing the african-american community... With laughter.
jokes.setup: Serious funeral eulogy opening about obesity
jokes.punchline: Undercutting seriousness with 'with laughter' addition
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy structure - serious social issue undercut by entertainment focus, fits Tracy's character

jokes.text: It's 67 minutes of me acting like a hilarious fool.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why he doesn't want movie released
jokes.punchline: Being upset about appearing hilarious, which is his actual talent
jokes.explanation: Good character irony - Tracy rejecting what he's naturally best at for pretentious artistic credibility

jokes.text: I had to go on Charlie Rose, Kenneth, Charlie Rose! Oh, that's horrible.
jokes.setup: Tracy describing his serious acting obligations
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's genuine horror at Charlie Rose appearance
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Kenneth's innocent reaction treating highbrow interview show as torture

jokes.text: From now on, the only movies Tracy Jordan makes are about the holocaust, Georgia O'Keeffe, or both.
jokes.setup: Tracy committing to serious dramatic roles only
jokes.punchline: The bizarre combination of Holocaust and Georgia O'Keeffe in one film
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation - the 'or both' creates an impossible and hilarious film concept

jokes.text: I didn't care much for the gazpacho soup. I mean, where's the fun of sending it back because it isn't hot?
jokes.setup: Colleen complaining about cold soup
jokes.punchline: Missing the point that gazpacho is supposed to be cold, focused on complaining opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Colleen character moment - sophisticated ignorance combined with need to complain about everything

jokes.text: What are my chums at the death shore retirement community gonna say... When I tell them that my unmarried son has knocked up a protestant?
jokes.setup: Colleen being upset about pregnancy news
jokes.punchline: 'Death shore' as name for retirement community, plus casual use of 'knocked up'
jokes.explanation: Excellent character writing - 'death shore' is darkly funny, combined with Colleen's crude language about pregnancy

jokes.text: It should have gone to the other boy!
jokes.setup: Jack bringing up high school graduation memory
jokes.punchline: Colleen immediately undermining his achievement decades later
jokes.explanation: Perfect mother-son dysfunction - Colleen's inability to be supportive even decades later

jokes.text: Most people thought I was a hero for killing Lydia's parrot.
jokes.setup: Colleen defending her past actions
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of parrot murder presented as heroic
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Colleen moment - treating pet murder as community service with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry. I thought you were a transvestite.
jokes.setup: Liz mistaking Paul (in restaurant uniform) for someone else
jokes.punchline: Specific and inappropriate assumption
jokes.explanation: Weak Liz moment - relies on random insensitivity without much craft or character insight

jokes.text: The coverage preempted a tampon commercial she was in.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Jenna was upset about Hurricane Katrina
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was only because it affected her commercial, not the tragedy itself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna characterization - making natural disaster about her career setback, darkly funny and character-specific

jokes.text: I don't think about kissing her or laughing with her or photographing her with just salamanders covering her nipples.
jokes.setup: Paul claiming he doesn't think about Jenna anymore
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific romantic memories ending with bizarre salamander photography
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating structure - starts normal then gets increasingly specific and absurd, perfect for Jenna's artistic pretensions

jokes.text: You can't be on the floor without your roller skates, Rick. I'm not Rick.
jokes.setup: Manager giving work instructions
jokes.punchline: Wrong person entirely, highlighting absurd workplace (roller skating restaurant)
jokes.explanation: Decent background gag establishing the restaurant's quirky nature but not particularly strong

jokes.text: Welcome to my Christmas Attack Zone.
jokes.setup: Jack greeting Liz at dinner
jokes.punchline: Militaristic framing of family dinner as warfare
jokes.explanation: Good Jack phrase - treating family gathering like military operation, sets up episode's conflict nicely

jokes.text: I could be sitting at the corner table at the Kmart Cafe right now.
jokes.setup: Liz complaining about drama at Jack's dinner
jokes.punchline: Referencing her earlier pathetic Christmas Eve plans as preferable
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces Liz's sad lifestyle choices as somehow appealing compared to family drama

jokes.text: But I once saw Colleen provoke a Buddhist monk into whipping a battery at her.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining that Milton is a pacifist
jokes.punchline: Colleen's ability to anger even the most peaceful person
jokes.explanation: Exceptional character writing - Buddhist monk battery-throwing is perfectly absurd while illustrating Colleen's toxic personality

jokes.text: Oh, Jack, this is going to be the best winter's Eve light festival ever.
jokes.setup: Milton arriving for Christmas dinner
jokes.punchline: Hippie reframing of Christmas as generic winter celebration
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - Milton's hippie nature through holiday rename, but not particularly clever

jokes.text: 'Joy, beautiful spark of the gods, daughter of Elysium.' And, Liz, you're already showing.
jokes.setup: Milton being told about pregnancy, seeing Liz
jokes.punchline: Assuming Liz is pregnant because of her normal body shape
jokes.explanation: Standard body humor - not particularly clever and relies on cheap shot at Liz's appearance

jokes.text: It's a girl! She'll be intuitive.
jokes.setup: Learning baby's gender
jokes.punchline: Hippie assumption about gender-based mystical abilities
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Milton's new-age beliefs but joke is fairly predictable

jokes.text: By the way, we have a tradition in my family where we let the child name itself. Oh, yeah, that's hippie nonsense. Absolutely not. Well, suit yourself, but my son Spider-Man turned out just fine.
jokes.setup: Milton suggesting family naming tradition
jokes.punchline: Revealing his son is actually named Spider-Man
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - sets up hippie parenting then delivers completely absurd but logical result

jokes.text: I once french-kissed a dog at a party to try to impress what turned out to be a very tall 12-year-old.
jokes.setup: Liz saying everyone makes mistakes
jokes.punchline: Increasingly horrifying personal anecdote
jokes.explanation: Exceptional cringe comedy - starts bad and gets exponentially worse with each detail, perfectly Liz

jokes.text: It was designed by M.C. Escher. These stairs are weird.
jokes.setup: Jack warning about confusing east wing
jokes.punchline: Liz immediately experiencing impossible architecture
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy concept - rich person having impossible art as functional architecture

jokes.text: I certainly wasn't going to pretend those trash cans were my parents.
jokes.setup: Kenneth denying he's setting up Christmas celebration at work
jokes.punchline: Revealing his backup plan was even sadder
jokes.explanation: Dark and sweet Kenneth moment - his innocent loneliness expressed through absurd makeshift family

jokes.text: Russell Crowe is having an auction to benefit the victims of his own mood swings.
jokes.setup: Tracy mentioning celebrity charity work
jokes.punchline: Russell Crowe raising money for people he's harmed through anger
jokes.explanation: Brilliant celebrity satire - takes Crowe's reputation and creates perfect absurd charity concept

jokes.text: In the Darfur region, the dead may be the lucky ones.
jokes.setup: Tracy showing serious movie to women's shelter
jokes.punchline: Incredibly inappropriate content for the venue
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy showing Tracy's complete tone-deafness about appropriate content for vulnerable audience

jokes.text: Except for Insulin, spironolactone, and bupropion, which I have for you whenever you're ready, sir.
jokes.setup: Tracy saying laughter is best medicine
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's literal medical correction with specific medications
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth deadpan - literal interpretation plus mysterious access to prescription drugs

jokes.text: My gift to you is the feeling of superiority you'll have for the next two seconds.
jokes.setup: Avery greeting Colleen at Christmas
jokes.punchline: Promising brief superiority before revealing bigger scandal
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup for reveal - Avery's confidence and precise timing create perfect dramatic irony

jokes.text: Because, you know, when you're pregnant, one bottle of wine a day, and that's it.
jokes.setup: Avery asking if Colleen is drunk
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of drinking during pregnancy as if it's moderation
jokes.explanation: Good Avery character moment showing her wealthy privilege and casual disregard for health advice

jokes.text: He got me a kidney... From Elvis Costello.
jokes.setup: Colleen saying Jack is a good boy
jokes.punchline: Bizarre specific example involving celebrity organ donation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist detail - casual mention of impossible celebrity medical procedure as motherly pride

jokes.text: Going on father-and-son habitat for humanity builds and road trips in my V.W. van.
jokes.setup: Milton listing missed experiences with Jack
jokes.punchline: Perfectly stereotypical hippie father activities
jokes.explanation: Predictable hippie father clichés - fits character but not particularly clever writing

jokes.text: I want to eat shrimp off an old gay dressed as baby new year.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining why the party matters
jokes.punchline: Incredibly specific and inappropriate party fantasy
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully weird Jenna moment - the specificity makes it both funny and disturbing

jokes.text: Welcome to my Christmas Attack Zone.
jokes.setup: Colleen planning her next move
jokes.punchline: Adopting Jack's militaristic phrase for her own warfare
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Colleen adopting Jack's strategy, escalating the family warfare

jokes.text: Ladies of the battered women's shelter, please be quiet, a man is talking.
jokes.setup: Tracy addressing shelter audience
jokes.punchline: Incredibly inappropriate domineering behavior at vulnerable women's shelter
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe comedy - Tracy's complete tone-deafness about audience and setting

jokes.text: You're gonna see poverty, drug abuse, and a bunch of babies having a hammer fight in a dumpster.
jokes.setup: Tracy describing his serious dramatic film
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and inappropriate content description
jokes.explanation: Great escalating structure - builds to completely absurd 'baby hammer fight' that sounds like bad exploitation film

jokes.text: Actually, he's behind you, Tracy. He rode over here with us. You talked to him the whole ride.
jokes.setup: Tracy saying he can't get Kenneth out of his head
jokes.punchline: Revealing Kenneth is literally physically present
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment showing Tracy's complete lack of awareness of his surroundings

jokes.text: I give you The Chunks 2: A very chunky Christmas.
jokes.setup: Tracy deciding to show his comedy instead
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous sequel title
jokes.explanation: Decent callback with appropriately silly sequel title but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I had a couple of rich men die on top of me.
jokes.setup: Colleen describing heart attack symptoms
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of multiple wealthy lovers' deaths during intimacy
jokes.explanation: Exceptional dark comedy - casual mention of multiple deaths during sex, delivered with complete matter-of-factness

jokes.text: Listen to me, damn it. I'm a doctor. Of history.
jokes.setup: Milton asserting medical authority
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's completely unqualified for medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - builds medical authority then completely deflates it with useless doctorate

jokes.text: In what emergency would you be necessary... If someone wanted to know whether the '60s were awesome or not? They were.
jokes.setup: Jack questioning Milton's emergency usefulness
jokes.punchline: Milton immediately proving point with irrelevant hippie nostalgia
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Milton immediately validating Jack's criticism with exactly the behavior described

jokes.text: Seems like people are back on my side, Jackie.
jokes.setup: Family rallying around Colleen's medical emergency
jokes.punchline: Colleen viewing health crisis as strategic advantage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Colleen manipulation - treating medical emergency as tactical maneuver in family warfare

jokes.text: You dress as Natalie Portman from the movie Black Swan, and I dress as former Pittsburgh Steelers Wide Receiver and Pennsylvania Gubernatorial Nominee Lynn Swann. We're two black swans.
jokes.setup: Paul and Jenna revealing couple's costume ideas
jokes.punchline: Incredibly elaborate pun connecting movie to obscure football player
jokes.explanation: Brilliant elaborate pun - connects current movie to obscure political/sports figure through 'swan' wordplay, perfectly overly-complex

jokes.text: I have a crush on the mentalist.
jokes.setup: Family sharing secrets honestly
jokes.punchline: Liz's 'secret' being pathetic TV crush
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces Liz's sad lifestyle but not particularly clever on its own

jokes.text: I'm gonna go get a bus to white haven now, and I should be home just in time for aunt Linda to try to prove that she's sober by holding someone's baby while cooking.
jokes.setup: Liz deciding to join her family after all
jokes.punchline: Incredibly specific and dangerous family dysfunction scenario
jokes.explanation: Great callback with escalated specificity - the baby-holding while cooking detail is perfectly dangerous and dysfunctional

jokes.text: Just my mom and dad yelling at me together.
jokes.setup: Milton asking why Jack is smiling
jokes.punchline: Jack finding joy in family dysfunction because it feels normal
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Jack finding twisted comfort in unified parental disapproval, touching and funny

jokes.text: You insult your own intelligence.
jokes.setup: Family argument continuing
jokes.punchline: Colleen's elegant yet confusing insult
jokes.explanation: Good Colleen line - sounds sophisticated but doesn't quite make sense, typical of her character

jokes.text: You are a war criminal. I hate to say it.
jokes.setup: Family argument escalating during Christmas carol
jokes.punchline: Extreme accusation delivered matter-of-factly during holy song
jokes.explanation: Good contrast comedy - serious accusation during peaceful Christmas carol, typical Colleen escalation

jokes.text: And somebody needs to clean this table up. It's disgusting.
jokes.setup: Tracy ending heartfelt Christmas message
jokes.punchline: Immediately switching to mundane complaint about mess
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of sentimental moment with practical complaint. Fits Tracy's inability to stay serious for long.
